data.title: Price target for Optimism (OP) predicted at $0.13-$0.15 by March end, with potential gains of 8-25% despite bearish momentum.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about the price prediction for Optimism (OP), expecting potential gains of 8-25% to reach a range between $0.13 to $0.15 by the end of the month. This positive outlook comes despite the current presence of bearish technical signals. The analysts are focusing on a key resistance breakout as a potential catalyst for this upward trend.

data.title: Best AI Course Online (2026): Coursera Named Go-To Platform for Artificial Intelligence Training by Consumer365
data.description: On March 14, 2026, Consumer365 identified Coursera as a top choice for artificial intelligence training in its Best AI Course Online (2026) report. Coursera was commended for its wide range of AI courses created in collaboration with universities and tech companies. This recognition emphasizes Coursera's leading position in the industry as a go-to platform for AI education.

data.description: The cryptocurrency Arbitrum (ARB) is currently being traded at $0.10 as it faces a bearish market trend. Analysis indicates a likelihood of a drop to $0.09 support level before starting an upward trend aiming for the $0.12 resistance level in the second quarter of 2026.

Scott A Kupor has recently made public his latest stock transactions.

data.title: These Analysts Slash Their Forecasts On Sleep Number Following Q4 Earnings
data.description: Sleep Number Corporation (SNBR) surpassed expectations in its fourth-quarter earnings report. The company reported losses of 46 cents per share, exceeding the predicted 50 cents per share. Sales totaled $347.385 million, surpassing estimates of $328.668 million. CEO Linda Findley stated, "Sleep Number exceeded guidance for 2025 and continues to make strides in new product and marketing strategies while cutting costs. The pro-forma adjusted EBITDA margin for 2025 was around 9%." This information was obtained from a report on earnings released by the company.

data.title: ATOM may reach $2.40 by April 2026, bouncing from $1.87 supported by RSI divergence and defense, per analysts.
data.description: With Cosmos (ATOM) currently trading at $1.87, analysts are observing a potential oversold bounce. Technical analysis indicates a recovery target of $2.40 over the next 4-6 weeks due to RSI divergence and support levels being defended.

data.title: UNI expected to hit $4.20 in March 2026. Priced at $3.94 with targets of $4.15-$4.22. Breakout likely above $4.12.
data.description: Uniswap's (UNI) current price stands at $3.94, displaying neutral momentum as analysts set their sights on targets between $4.15 and $4.22. Technical indicators point towards a possible breakout above the $4.12 resistance level.

data.title: Vivid Seats Analysts Cut Their Forecasts After Q4 Loss
data.description: Vivid Seats Inc. (NASDAQ: SEAT) reported a fourth-quarter loss with a quarterly net loss of $428.7 million, compared to a $424.2 million loss the previous year. Revenues dropped by 37% year-over-year to $126.8 million from $199.8 million. Lawrence Fey, the CEO of Vivid Seats, mentioned that the first-quarter trends validate their strategy and execution, bringing tangible results. The company is focusing on improving its technology, data assets, operational efficiency, and unique customer value proposition. The enhanced app has shown promising results, contributing positively to the company's momentum. For the full article, check on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BCH expected to rebound to $485-$500 by April 2026 after hitting oversold signals at $458 with RSI at 41.15. Technical analysis indicates a possible bounce within 4-6 weeks if key support is maintained.
data.description: Bitcoin Cash is currently experiencing oversold signals at a price point of $458, as indicated by the Relative Strength Index (RSI) at 41.15. Based on technical analysis, it is anticipated that there could be a rebound towards the $485-$500 range in the next 4-6 weeks, provided that crucial support levels are maintained.

data.title: These Analysts Lower Their Forecasts On Gambling.com Following Q4 Earnings
data.description: Gambling.com Group Limited (NASDAQ: GAMB) exceeded expectations in Q4 with earnings per share of 30 cents, surpassing the analyst estimate of 24 cents. Quarterly sales amounted to $46.236 million, higher than the anticipated $46.057 million. For FY2026, the company anticipates sales between $170.00 million and $180.00 million, contrasting with market projections of $185.309 million. CEO and Co-Founder Charles Gillespie stated, "We achieved record Q4 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA, with revenue climbing 31% year-over-year to $46.2 million and Adjusted EBITDA up 5% to $15.5 million. The sports data services segment notably expanded by 29% quarterly, constituting 26% of total revenue."

data.title: Obstacle for Fed Chair pick as Justice Dept. threat complicates Fed investigation with possible appeal for Kevin Warsh.
data.description: A probe into the activities of the Federal Reserve hit a roadblock on Friday, potentially hindering progress. However, an appeal by the department could further impede Kevin Warsh's actions.

data.title: Switching addresses resulted in losing Medicare coverage for one woman, raising concerns about the impact of using a post office box.
data.description: A woman's health insurance was jeopardized due to using a post office box. This unexpected consequence highlights a concerning issue in the system.

data.title: LINK price forecast sees $10.50-$12.00 by April 2026, signaling 15-32% upside from $9.01. Analysts bullish on Chainlink.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about the bullish potential of Chainlink (LINK), predicting a target range of $10.50 to $12.00 in the near future. The current technical analysis indicates a potential upside of 15-32% from the current $9.01 level.

data.title: Regional rivalries and geography hinder efforts to address oil disruption in the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict.
data.description: Geographical factors and rivalries among regions have hindered Gulf countries from identifying a viable substitute for the strait. The ongoing conflict with Iran has led to the closure of the strait, significantly impacting transportation routes for vital resources.

data.title: US's reliance on oil has reduced thanks to energy efficiency and renewables, driving down household budgets.
data.description: Gasoline continues to play a significant role in household budgets, but advancements in energy efficiency and increased use of renewables have led to a decrease in the economy's overall dependence on petroleum. This shift has resulted in a notable reduction in the consumption of gasoline, highlighting a move towards more sustainable energy sources. This change is reflected in key data points such as declining petroleum usage in various sectors and an increase in the adoption of cleaner energy alternatives. As a result, households and industries are becoming less reliant on gasoline as technology and renewable energy sources continue to reshape the energy landscape.
